 {"id":"franken_networkx-2u4","title":"Extract flow algorithm epsilon values to named constants","description":"## What

Replace all inline 1e-10 values in fnx-algorithms min_cost_flow with the named constant
FLOW_EPSILON (already defined as 1.0e-10 at the top of lib.rs).

## Status: COMPLETED (March 2026)

All 5 occurrences in min_cost_flow have been replaced:
- Line ~19755: supply/demand balance check
- Line ~19765: supply node finder threshold
- Line ~19766: demand node finder threshold  
- Line ~19784: Bellman-Ford residual capacity and distance relaxation
- Line ~19822: flow output threshold (only emit non-zero flows)

## Why FLOW_EPSILON specifically

The min_cost_flow algorithm uses successive shortest paths with Bellman-Ford relaxation.
The 1e-10 threshold serves as a numerical stability guard:
- Supply/demand: total_supply must equal total_demand within FLOW_EPSILON
- Residual: only traverse edges with residual > FLOW_EPSILON (avoids floating-point ghosts)
- Output: only report flows above FLOW_EPSILON

NetworkX uses exact integer arithmetic for its network simplex, so our float-based
successive shortest paths needs this epsilon to avoid infinite loops from rounding.

## Files Changed

- crates/fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs (lines 25, ~19755, ~19765, ~19766, ~19784, ~19822)

## Verification

- cargo test -p fnx-algorithms passes (595 tests)
- Python test suite passes (1082+ tests)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:31.541611556Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:01.146759523Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:01.146045561Z","close_reason":"All 5 inline 1e-10 values replaced with FLOW_EPSILON constant. Verified: 595 Rust tests + 1082 Python tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-2zl","title":"Small Utilities: eulerize, moral_graph, equivalence_classes, misc","description":"## Scope
Implement ~15 small utility functions:
- eulerize(G): add minimum edges to make graph Eulerian. Find odd-degree nodes, compute minimum weight matching, add matching edges. O(V^2) for matching.
- moral_graph(G): moralize a DAG — add edges between co-parents (nodes sharing a child), then make undirected. Used in Bayesian networks.
- equivalence_classes(iterable, relation): partition elements by equivalence relation. Union-find based, O(n * alpha(n)).
- chordless_cycles(G): find all chordless (induced) cycles. Each cycle has no chord connecting non-adjacent vertices.
- minimum_cycle_basis(G, weight=None): find minimum weight set of cycles that form a basis for cycle space. Uses Horton's algorithm, O(V * E^2).
- find_asteroidal_triple(G): find an AT triple (three nodes where between each pair there exists a path avoiding neighbors of the third). O(V^3).
- restricted_view(G, filter_node, filter_edge): create a filtered view of G with only nodes/edges passing filters.
- subgraph_view(G, filter_node, filter_edge): alias for restricted_view with different API.
- reverse(G) / reverse_view(G): reverse edges of a DiGraph. reverse creates a copy, reverse_view creates a view.
- to_directed(G) / to_undirected(G): global conversion functions.
- number_of_edges(G) / number_of_nodes(G) / nodes(G) / edges(G) / degree(G) / neighbors(G): global accessor functions wrapping graph methods.

## Test Requirements
- eulerize: verify result is Eulerian (all even degree, connected)
- moral_graph: verify co-parents are connected, verify undirected
- equivalence_classes: verify partition property (complete, disjoint)
- chordless_cycles: verify no cycle has a chord
- minimum_cycle_basis: verify independence, verify spanning cycle space
- NX cross-validation on all functions","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-22T02:19:47.942297731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T22:43:05.144660756Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T22:43:05.144136194Z","close_reason":"Implemented 13 small utilities: eulerize, moral_graph, equivalence_classes, minimum_cycle_basis, chordless_cycles, to_directed, to_undirected, reverse, nodes, edges, degree, number_of_nodes, number_of_edges.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithms","tier-4"]}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-uhq","title":"Remaining Utilities: graph products, k-truss, k-factor, Voronoi, SNAP aggregation","description":"## Scope
Implement ~40 miscellaneous utility functions:

### Graph Products
- `corona_product` — corona product of two graphs
- `modular_product` — modular product
- `rooted_product` — rooted product

### Structural Analysis
- `k_truss` — k-truss subgraph (maximal subgraph where every edge in at least k-2 triangles)
- `k_factor` — find k-factor (k-regular spanning subgraph)
- `k_components` — k-component structure (Moody-White decomposition)
- `onion_layers` — onion decomposition (k-core layers with additional structure)

### Voronoi & Partitioning
- `voronoi_cells` — Voronoi partition of graph based on center nodes
- `snap_aggregation` — SNAP graph aggregation (summarize graph by node grouping)

### Matching & Covering
- `min_edge_dominating_set` — minimum edge dominating set (NP-hard, approximation)
- `min_weighted_dominating_set` — minimum weighted dominating set
- `maximum_independent_set` — maximum independent set (NP-hard)
- `max_weight_clique` — maximum weight clique
- `ramsey_R2` — find Ramsey partition into clique and independent set

### Flows & Cuts
- `minimum_edge_cut` — min edge cut (might already exist, verify)
- `minimum_node_cut` — min node cut
- `stoer_wagner` — Stoer-Wagner min-cut algorithm for undirected graphs

### Chordality & Structure
- `is_chordal` — test if graph is chordal
- `chordal_graph_cliques` — cliques of chordal graph (perfect elimination ordering)
- `chordal_graph_treewidth` — treewidth of chordal graph

### Bridges & Chains
- `chain_decomposition` — decompose graph into chains (related to ear decomposition)
- `has_bridges` — quickly check if graph has any bridge

### Small-World
- `random_reference` — random graph with same degree sequence (for sigma/omega)
- `lattice_reference` — lattice graph with same degree sequence

### Miscellaneous
- `square_clustering` — local clustering variant counting squares
- `generalized_degree` — degree considering triangles
- `rich_club_coefficient` — rich-club coefficient by degree threshold
- `average_neighbor_degree` — average degree of neighbors
- `effective_size` — Burt's effective size (structural holes)
- `constraint` — Burt's constraint measure
- `local_efficiency` — average inverse shortest path of node neighborhood
- `global_efficiency` — average inverse shortest path length
- `min_edge_cover` — minimum edge cover
- `is_edge_cover` — verify edge cover
- `boundary_expansion` — vertex/edge boundary
- `node_boundary` / `edge_boundary` — boundary sets
- `dispersion` — dispersion of node pair (Facebook metric)
- `closeness_vitality` — Wiener index change on node removal

## Implementation Location
- Python: various modules under `python/franken_networkx/algorithms/`
- k_truss and k_components could benefit from Rust

## Test Requirements
- Cross-validate against NetworkX for each function
- Property tests for graph products (verify product graph size = V1*V2 or similar)
- NP-hard functions: test on small instances only","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:15:02.771609031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T07:35:53.952958615Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T07:35:53.952429654Z","close_reason":"All sub-beads closed: products (69m), decomposition (3r3), utilities (64l, 2zl). Utility expansion complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithms","tier-4","utilities"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_networkx-uhq","depends_on_id":"franken_networkx-hp3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:16:02.923936917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-ulw","title":"Spectral & Matrix: Bethe Hessian, directed Laplacian, Google matrix","description":"## Scope
Implement 7 spectral/matrix functions:
- `bethe_hessian_matrix` — Bethe Hessian matrix (deformed graph Laplacian, used in community detection)
- `bethe_hessian_spectrum` — eigenvalues of Bethe Hessian
- `directed_laplacian_matrix` — Laplacian for directed graphs (uses PageRank-based transition matrix)
- `directed_combinatorial_laplacian_matrix` — combinatorial Laplacian variant for digraphs
- `google_matrix` — Google's PageRank matrix (stochastic + damping)
- `normalized_laplacian_spectrum` — eigenvalues of normalized Laplacian
- `attr_matrix` — construct matrix from node/edge attributes

## Algorithmic Approach
- bethe_hessian_matrix: H(r) = (r^2 - 1) * I - r * A + D, where A is adjacency, D is degree diagonal, r is regularizer (default: sqrt of average degree).
- directed_laplacian_matrix: Compute stationary distribution of random walk (PageRank), form L = Phi - (Phi*P + P^T*Phi)/2.
- google_matrix: G = alpha * S + (1-alpha) * (1/N) * E, where S is stochastic adjacency.
- normalized_laplacian_spectrum: eigenvalues of D^{-1/2} L D^{-1/2}.
- attr_matrix: Build matrix from edge attribute function, rows/columns ordered by node list.

## Dependencies
- Depends on existing spectral functions in the project
- Requires scipy.sparse for efficient matrix operations

## Implementation Location
- Python: `python/franken_networkx/linalg/` — extend existing spectral module

## Test Requirements
- Verify matrix properties (symmetry, positive semidefiniteness where expected)
- Verify dimensions match graph size
- Cross-validate eigenvalues against NetworkX
- Test on known graphs with known spectra (complete graph, cycle, path)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:13:28.685600348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T19:41:04.703392816Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T19:41:04.702903159Z","close_reason":"Implemented 7 spectral/matrix functions: bethe_hessian_matrix, bethe_hessian_spectrum, google_matrix, normalized_laplacian_spectrum, directed_laplacian_matrix, directed_combinatorial_laplacian_matrix, attr_matrix. Total: 455 functions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithms","spectral","tier-3"]}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-v3y","title":"Advanced Centrality: current-flow betweenness, subset betweenness, Laplacian centrality","description":"## Scope
Implement ~20 advanced centrality functions:

### Current-Flow Betweenness Family
- `current_flow_betweenness_centrality` — based on electrical current flow
- `edge_current_flow_betweenness_centrality` — edge variant
- `approximate_current_flow_betweenness_centrality` — randomized approximation
- `current_flow_closeness_centrality` — closeness based on effective resistance

### Subset Betweenness Family
- `betweenness_centrality_subset` — betweenness restricted to source/target subsets
- `edge_betweenness_centrality_subset` — edge variant of subset betweenness

### Other Centrality Measures
- `edge_load_centrality` — load centrality for edges
- `laplacian_centrality` — based on Laplacian spectrum drop
- `percolation_centrality` — based on percolation states
- `information_centrality` — based on information flow (related to current-flow)
- `second_order_centrality` — based on random walk return times
- `subgraph_centrality` — based on subgraph counts (matrix exponential)
- `subgraph_centrality_exp` — explicit matrix exponential computation
- `communicability_betweenness_centrality` — based on communicability
- `estrada_index` — trace of matrix exponential (related to subgraph centrality)
- `trophic_levels` — trophic level in food webs
- `trophic_differences` — differences between connected trophic levels
- `trophic_incoherence_parameter` — measure of trophic incoherence
- `group_betweenness_centrality` — betweenness for a group of nodes
- `group_closeness_centrality` — closeness for a group of nodes

## Algorithmic Approach
- Current-flow variants: Solve Laplacian linear systems (L * x = b). Use scipy.sparse.linalg.spsolve or conjugate gradient. The Laplacian pseudoinverse gives effective resistances.
- Subset betweenness: Modify standard betweenness to only count paths from source set to target set.
- Laplacian centrality: Compute Laplacian eigenvalues, measure spectral impact of node removal.
- Percolation centrality: Weighted variant of betweenness using percolation states.
- Second-order centrality: Analyze variance of random walk return times.
- Subgraph centrality / communicability: Compute matrix exponential exp(A), diagonal entries give subgraph centrality.

## Dependencies
- Requires spectral/matrix functions (already implemented)
- numpy/scipy for linear algebra

## Test Requirements
- Cross-validate against NetworkX for small graphs (exact computation is expensive)
- Verify symmetry properties (undirected graphs)
- Verify normalization
- Performance benchmarks for approximate variants","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:11:48.521610696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T18:49:30.019156587Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T18:49:30.018635140Z","close_reason":"Implemented 18 advanced centrality functions: current_flow_betweenness (+ edge + approx + closeness), betweenness/edge_betweenness_subset, edge_load, laplacian, percolation, information, second_order, subgraph_exp, communicability_betweenness, trophic_levels/differences/incoherence, group_betweenness/closeness. Total: 579.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithms","centrality","tier-2"]}
